claimText: The PixxelSpace India-led Consortium won the IN-SPACe proposal to build India's first fully indigenous commercial Earth Observation Satellite System (EOSS).
evidenceExcerpt: PixxelSpace India-led Consortium won the IN-SPACe proposal to build India's first fully indigenous commercial Earth Observation Satellite System (EOSS) under the EO-PPP model.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the entity responsible for India's first commercial EOSS.

claimText: Under the EO-PPP model, the private sector is responsible for designing, building, and operating the satellites, while the government provides strategic and technical support.
evidenceExcerpt: EO-PPP model: Private sector designs, builds, operates; government provides strategic/technical support.
examUse: Statement-based question on the division of responsibilities in the EO-PPP model.
distractorHooks: Government builds and operates while private sector funds
Private sector only provides launch services

claimText: India's first commercial EOSS constellation will consist of 12 satellites featuring panchromatic, multispectral, hyperspectral, and microwave SAR sensors.
evidenceExcerpt: The constellation of 12 EO satellites will feature panchromatic, multispectral, hyperspectral, and microwave SAR sensors.
examUse: Feature-based question on the payload and size of the EOSS constellation.

claimText: Earth Observation (EO) satellites are used for applications including disaster management, crop estimation, mineralogy mapping, and oceanography.
evidenceExcerpt: EO satellite applications include disaster management, crop estimation, mineralogy mapping, and oceanography.
examUse: Application-based multiple-choice question on the uses of EO satellites.

claimText: Dhruva Space's LEAP-1 mission and Pixxel's Firefly satellites were launched aboard the SpaceX NAOS Falcon 9 mission.
evidenceExcerpt: Pixxel successfully launched three new Firefly satellites aboard the SpaceX NAOS Falcon 9 mission. Dhruva Space's LEAP-1 mission launched aboard SpaceX NAOS Falcon 9.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching private Indian space missions to their launch vehicles.
distractorHooks: LEAP-1 is a mission by Agnikul Cosmos
